title: campsiandra pasibensis
intro: Campsiandra pasibensis, also known as Pasib River tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the Philippines and has been utilized for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Campsiandra pasibensis include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to protect against cancer and cardiovascular diseases. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or decoction, and it can also be used in the form of a tincture or topical ointment.
